research_gaps.text: Traceability gap: agents may produce ideas without an auditable chain from source evidence to experiment decision.
Review gap: simulated reviewer feedback is often generated after the fact instead of controlling whether an idea proceeds.
Experiment timing gap: many research agents run experiments late, after committing to an idea, rather than using cheap probes during ideation.
Failure memory gap: rejected ideas and failed probes are often lost instead of shaping the next search iteration.
Venue-fit gap: agent outputs often lack explicit comparison to workshop and conference expectations.

idea_tree.title: Evidence-to-Experiment Trace Ledger for traceable autonomous research
idea_tree.pitch: Build and test evidence-to-experiment trace ledger so research agents must connect traceable, autonomous, research, agents to review and experiment decisions before claims advance.
idea_tree.mechanism: The mechanism is to convert weak natural-language research judgement into explicit state, reviewer, and metric objects. This makes progress falsifiable because an idea must improve a defined local selection or validation metric before it is promoted.

fatal_flaws: The idea is not fully specified and lacks clear differentiation from existing work
The mechanism described appears to be an incremental extension of existing agent-based research systems
fixable_flaws: Needs more specificity about how 'venue-aware claim narrowing' differs from existing approaches
Requires clearer definition of the conversion from natural-language judgment to explicit objects
Needs to articulate what makes this approach novel compared to The AI Scientist series and other agent-based research systems
required_experiments: Demonstrate the venue-aware claim narrowing mechanism on a concrete research task
Compare the guided_selection_utility_minus_random_baseline against existing approaches
Show how the explicit state/reviewer/metric objects improve falsifiability over natural-language judgment
missing_related_work: Cite recent work on claim verification and narrowing in automated reasoning
Reference papers on explicit representation of research decisions and metrics
Include work on venue-aware or context-aware AI systems
reviewers.justification: The idea builds on existing work in automated scientific discovery but introduces a specific mechanism for venue-aware claim narrowing. However, it lacks specificity and needs to be more clearly defined and differentiated from existing work to be considered novel.
